verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN48/TGN51) is a type I integral membrane protein that resides in the trans-Golgi network and cycles between early endosomes and the TGN. Its canonical localization is endomembrane — TGN and endosomes — not the plasma membrane outer leaflet. While it cycles through intracellular trafficking pathways, there is no documented stable surface pool on the outer face of the plasma membrane, no GPI anchor, and no evidence of cell-state-induced or cancer-related ecto-display. It functions as a TGN sorting/trafficking protein, not a plasma membrane receptor.
